channel.title: Apocalypse on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Apocalypse on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Nibiru cataclysm
item.description:
The Nibiru cataclysm is a predicted disastrous encounter between Earth and a large planetary object, often referred to as Nibiru or Planet X. This doomsday scenario was first proposed in 1995 by Nancy Lieder, who claimed to receive messages from extraterrestrials. The idea has since spread through various internet groups and has been linked to the 2012 phenomenon and other astronomical events. Astronomers and planetary scientists have widely rejected the Nibiru cataclysm as pseudoscience, stating there is no scientific evidence to support such claims.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nibiru_cataclysm

item.title: Fiat Lux
item.description:
Fiat Lux is a neo-revelationist UFO religion founded in 1980 by Erika Bertschinger-Eicke, who was known as Uriella. Members of the group, primarily located in Germany’s Black Forest, believe in the imminent apocalypse and the arrival of alien ships to evacuate adherents to a new paradise called “Amora.” Uriella, who claimed to receive divine messages, died in 2019. The organization has been described as a cult and has an estimated following of thousands.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fiat_Lux_%28UFO_religion%29

item.title: Chen Tao (UFO religion)
item.description:
Chen Tao, also known as God’s Salvation Church, was a UFO religion founded in Taiwan in 1996 by Hon-Ming Chen. The group relocated to Texas in 1997 and gained notoriety for a failed millennial prophecy. Chen Tao members wore white clothing and cowboy hats, and called their leader “Teacher Chen.” After their prophecy of God’s appearance did not occur, the group largely disbanded, with many members returning to Taiwan.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chen_Tao_%28UFO_religion%29
